description_long: This hardware store is a two-story commercial building with a prominent arched entrance on the right side of the front facade. The front features large rectangular windows on both floors, with the lower level also displaying a series of smaller, arched windows and a single door. The left side of the building includes a protruding rectangular structure, likely for storage or loading, and the roofline is flat with a slight elevation at the front right corner. The rear and side facades are plain, with minimal detailing and a single small opening at the back. The overall structure is boxy and solid, with architectural details suggesting early 20th-century urban commercial design. The building appears to be constructed primarily from masonry or concrete, with large window openings and a sheltered entryway.
description: A two-story hardware store with an arched entrance, large front windows, and a boxy, masonry exterior.
description_view_0: Rear view showing a plain wall with a small rectangular opening and minimal architectural detail.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting a protruding rectangular structure, likely for storage or loading, and a flat roof.
description_view_2: Right side view with a mostly featureless wall and a glimpse of the front's arched entrance.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the main entrance with an arch, large windows on both floors, and a series of smaller arched windows on the lower level.
attribute_storefront_layout: The storefront features a prominent arched entrance on the right, large rectangular windows on both floors, and a series of smaller arched windows and a door on the lower left. The facade is symmetrical with minimal exterior decoration.
